headline: Wall Street Is Quietly Pricing In $100 Oil, And These Two Energy Giants Are the Biggest Winners
summary: The smart money is making a very specific bet right now: both ExxonMobil and Chevron are being priced for an oil environment significantly richer than what we’re seeing today. The capital allocation decisions, production buildouts, and institutional re-ratings on both stocks tell a clear, unified story. The Institutional Signal Start with the stock moves. XOM ... headline: Exxon Mobil Reshapes Fuel Flows With Australia Exports And Venezuela Option
summary: Exxon Mobil initiated its first ever gasoline shipments to Australia after Middle East tensions led to a near halt in traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. The company is also assessing a return to Venezuela to pursue heavy oil operations using newer extraction technology. Together, these moves show Exxon Mobil adjusting fuel flows and supply sources in response to geopolitical disruptions. For investors watching NYSE:XOM, these operational decisions sit on top of a share price of $151.21...
